description_long: This kiosk features a classical architectural design with a prominent white domed roof supported by a circular arrangement of slender columns. The structure is open on all sides, with large arched entryways between each set of columns, allowing for easy access and visibility from every direction. The columns are evenly spaced and have simple bases and capitals, contributing to the elegant and timeless appearance. The dome is smooth and unadorned, made to resemble polished marble or stone, and sits atop a thick entablature that connects all the columns. There are no visible windows, doors, or signage, emphasizing the open and public nature of the structure. The overall form and material suggest durability and a design intended for parks, plazas, or other communal urban spaces.
description: A white domed kiosk with open arches and classical columns, constructed in a circular layout. The structure is elegant, open on all sides, and made to resemble marble or stone.
description_view_0: The domed roof and arched entryways are visible, with columns forming a circular perimeter; the open design is prominent.
description_view_1: The dome and arches are clearly seen, with the symmetry of the columns and open spaces emphasized from this angle.
description_view_2: The smooth dome and supporting columns are visible, highlighting the open, accessible nature of the structure.
description_view_3: The dome and circular arrangement of columns are apparent, with the open arches creating a sense of transparency and flow.
attribute_door_configuration: none; open archways on all sides
attribute_roof_design: Domed roof, smooth and unadorned, made of stone or marble, with no overhangs or decorative elements.
attribute_architectural_style: Classical, with Greco-Roman influences evident in the columns and domed roof.
